title: Earthquake bedside safety kit
summary: Prepare a small bedside set that reduces barefoot injuries, darkness, lost glasses, and delayed contact after night shaking.
content:
Prepare a small bedside set that reduces barefoot injuries, darkness, lost glasses, and delayed contact after night shaking.

Practical sequence: Place closed shoes, light, glasses, whistle, essential medicine card, and phone power where they can be reached without crossing broken glass. Identify the active hazard, official instruction, safest usable zone, exits, utilities, and people needing assistance for “Earthquake bedside safety kit”. Inspect from a safe position for smoke, heat, water, gas odour, sparks, falling objects, cracks, unstable surfaces, and blocked routes for “Earthquake bedside safety kit”. Move people early to the safer zone or leave when instructed; take medicines, communication, identification, water, and essential mobility aids for “Earthquake bedside safety kit”. Control only hazards that can be handled without entering smoke, floodwater, live electricity, gas, fire, or an unstable structure for “Earthquake bedside safety kit”. Mark unsafe areas, account for everyone, record changes, and reassess after every aftershock, weather shift, alarm, or utility change for “Earthquake bedside safety kit”. Safety limits: Official instructions and emergency services override “Earthquake bedside safety kit”; do not delay evacuation or urgent help to complete the card. Do not re-enter a restricted, burning, smoke-filled, flooded, gas-affected, electrically live, or structurally uncertain area in “Earthquake bedside safety kit”. Generators, grills, and fuel-burning devices must never operate inside homes, garages, or other enclosed occupied spaces in “Earthquake bedside safety kit”. Reduced fallback: If “flashlight and spare power for “Earthquake bedside safety kit”” is unavailable, use only a clean, labelled substitute that performs the same function; otherwise mark the missing capability and pause that step.

title: Aftershock waiting-zone selection
summary: Choose a temporary waiting place that avoids façades, glass, power lines, unstable walls, traffic, and blocked emergency access.
content:
Choose a temporary waiting place that avoids façades, glass, power lines, unstable walls, traffic, and blocked emergency access.

Working sequence: After the initial shaking, account for people and move only by a route that does not pass obvious falling-object, gas, fire, or electrical hazards. title: Post-earthquake stair and exit check
summary: Use a cautious visual exit check so people do not rush into damaged stairs, lifts, falling debris, smoke, or gas.
content:
Use a cautious visual exit check so people do not rush into damaged stairs, lifts, falling debris, smoke, or gas.

Field sequence: Do not use lifts; inspect the route from a safe position and keep everyone away when cracks, movement, smoke, water, sparks, or gas odour are present. title: Earthquake cabinet and falling-object control
summary: Reduce preventable indoor injuries by moving heavy items low and securing unstable furniture, shelves, tanks, and cabinet doors.
content:
Reduce preventable indoor injuries by moving heavy items low and securing unstable furniture, shelves, tanks, and cabinet doors.

Practical sequence: Prioritise objects above beds, exits, seating, children’s areas, and utility controls, then test that the fix does not create a new sharp, trip, or fire hazard. title: Earthquake re-entry boundary card
summary: Create a visible stop card that prevents casual re-entry into a visibly damaged or officially restricted building.
content:
Create a visible stop card that prevents casual re-entry into a visibly damaged or officially restricted building.

Working sequence: Treat new cracks, shifted supports, falling material, gas odour, fire, sparks, flooding, jammed exits, or official restriction as no-entry conditions. Identify the active hazard, official instruction, safest usable zone, exits, utilities, and people needing assistance for “Earthquake re-entry boundary card”. Inspect from a safe position for smoke, heat, water, gas odour, sparks, falling objects, cracks, unstable surfaces, and blocked routes for “Earthquake re-entry boundary card”. Safety limits: Official instructions and emergency services override “Earthquake re-entry boundary card”; do not delay evacuation or urgent help to complete the card. Do not re-enter a restricted, burning, smoke-filled, flooded, gas-affected, electrically live, or structurally uncertain area in “Earthquake re-entry boundary card”. Generators, grills, and fuel-burning devices must never operate inside homes, garages, or other enclosed occupied spaces in “Earthquake re-entry boundary card”.
